scope: Frontend-only productivity workspace. No backend APIs, authentication, permissions, reports, dashboards, Salesforce queries, or cache architecture were changed.

workspaceTabs.activeTabRestore: last active tab id is persisted and restored visually

apiUsage.commandPalette: Uses existing /api/search/global only after user enters at least two characters.

integrationPoints: openRecordDetail records recent items and workspace tabs after main record data renders.
Record detail header receives local Favorite and Pin actions.
Existing browser history remains the source for Back and Forward.
Existing SPA list/detail restore pipeline remains unchanged.

featuresImplemented: Generic recently viewed engine grouped by object.
Local favorites for records, reports, dashboards, objects, and workspace page targets through record header actions and Ctrl+K result stars.
Pinned records with quick access panel.
Workspace tabs with switch, close, browser Back, browser Forward, and last active tab persistence.
Breadcrumb-style workspace memory through existing route/history state plus workspace recent/tabs.
Ctrl+K command palette for objects, records, reports, dashboards, admin/settings, and commands.
Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+K, Alt+1 Accounts, Alt+2 Contacts, Alt+3 Leads, Alt+4 Opportunities, Alt+5 Cases, Escape, Ctrl+/.
Collapsible productivity panel containing Recently Viewed, Favorites, Pinned Records, and Keyboard Shortcuts.

performanceNotes.recordOpenImpact: Workspace state updates run after record data is available and do not block the main record render.
performanceNotes.commandSearch: User-initiated only; uses existing search endpoint.

manualScenarios: Open records and confirm recently viewed newest-first behavior.
Open multiple records and switch/close workspace tabs.
Use Alt+1 through Alt+5 object shortcuts.

remainingImprovements: Reports and dashboards can later receive their own page-level star buttons without changing APIs.
Command palette keyboard arrow navigation can be expanded beyond first-result Enter support.
Workspace tabs could later support explicit reuse/new-tab user preference.
